{{Quote|The gist of this track was, 'We are artists just like anyone else, we operate under the same freedom of speech laws as you do, and if you don't like our material that's just tough.'|Arts Correspondent "Ars Dangor," describing "Artistic Integrity"|<ref name="Latest Developments: The Emperor's New Clothes versus Deeply Religious">{{WEGCite|story=Latest Developments: The Emperor's New Clothes versus Deeply Religious|book=Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim}}</ref>}}
"'''Artistic Integrity'''" was a [[Music/Legends|song]] from the pro-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Band/Legends|musical group]] [[The Emperor's New Clothes]]' [[Artistic Integrity|eponymous album]]. "Artistic Integrity" was written as a response to the songs "[[Torch Song]]" and "[[Just Another Art Form]]" by the band [[Deeply Religious]], with whom The Emperor's New Clothes were engaged in a feud.

==History==
"Artistic Integrity" was the title track from the pro-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Music/Legends|musical]] [[Band/Legends|group]] [[The Emperor's New Clothes]]' album [[Artistic Integrity|of the same name]], the band's latest release at some point in [[Time/Legends|time]]<ref name="GG9" /> between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="Date">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'' is set in the [[Time/Legends|time period]] between the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which are dated to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], respectively, by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', which also establishes the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] as the final event before the beginning of [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].</ref> Like the rest of the group's music, "Artistic Integrity" was approved by the [[Imperial Board of Culture]], the Imperial organization charged with reviewing and censoring popular culture media.<ref name="GG9" /> The song was composed in the [[Minga (music)|minga]] genre<ref name="Genre" /> and written as a response to the songs "[[Torch Song]]" and "[[Just Another Art Form]]" by the band [[Deeply Religious]], after those two songs needled The Emperor's New Clothes.<ref name="Latest Developments">{{WEGCite|story=Latest Developments: The Emperor's New Clothes versus Deeply Religious|book=Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim}}</ref>

According to a ''[[Galactic Weekly NewsStack]]''{{'s}} arts correspondent—who was using the pseudonym [[Ars Dangor (correspondent)|Ars Dangor]]—"Artistic Integrity" was a very pointed track, which stated that The Emperor's New Clothes were free to make their own music, despite the fact that not everyone liked it. Deeply Religious continued the feud, releasing the short track "[[Ooh, It Bites]]," which Dangor considered "nasty." Since Deeply Religious had been banned by the Imperial Board of Culture, the Board did not review their work, and Dangor speculated that the conflict between the bands would go on.<ref name="Latest Developments" />

==Behind the scenes==
"Artistic Integrity" was mentioned in a [[1993]] supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', written by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann|Eric Trautmann]]. The song was elaborated upon in the book's segment titled "[[Latest Developments: The Emperor's New Clothes versus Deeply Religious]]," presented as an [[in-universe]] report by Ars Dangor.<ref name="GG9" />
